channel.title: EdTech on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in EdTech on RaihnForge

item.title: Alma Norwahl Sticker
item.description:
A sticker designed for the team at Alma, the cloud-based Student Information System built by educators for educators.

The piece riffs on a small detail you notice the moment you walk into Alma’s studio: the waiting room is full of narwhals. The narwhal is the right mascot for the company. It looks mythical, a fat happy dolphin with a unicorn horn, the kind of creature you’d swear had to be made up. But it’s a real animal that genuinely exists in the world. That’s the same shape as Alma’s positioning: a school-technology company that genuinely cares about teachers and students sounds like a thing somebody made up to sell a slide. Alma is the one that turns out to actually be real.

item.title: Kelvin Education — Brand Illustration
item.description:
Brand illustration system for Kelvin Education — mascot character, branded composition, and the visual context around the “Healthier, happier places to learn” promise.

The illustration carries across Kelvin’s web presence, presentation materials, video stills, social media, and product onboarding flows. The mascot anchors a friendly, approachable tone for an educational technology audience while staying consistent with the broader Kelvin brand system.

Role: Lead Production Designer · Kelvin Education

Live: kelvin.education

item.title: Kelvin RISE Microlearning — Content Redesign
item.description: Iterative content-structure redesign for Kelvin Education’s RISE microlearning sequence. The brief: a single lesson was running eleven content cards in the prototype version, which was more friction than the audience (kids) could carry, and the pacing felt diffuse. The work walks four density variations side by side — Prototype (11 cards), Efficient (7 cards), Adjust Initial (5 cards), and Adjust Merge (6 cards) — and lands on a final “Combined Ideas” rough draft that keeps the moments that earn their place and drops the ones that don’t.

item.title: Agile Project Plan: An EdTech Content Initiative
item.description:
Prepared for an EdTech employer. ADMG 479 — Project Management Maturity, for Professor Dr. Laura Williamson. Drafted by Joshua Keyes, June 5, 2025.

1. Project-Specific Information

The employer is exploring, through research and development, the creation of content that complements the data gathering it does to assess and monitor student wellness. This utilizes the learning-loop method. The four steps are Feedback, Reflection, Learning, and Application (Hubkengroup, 2025). The company’s survey platform constitutes the Feedback and Reflection portion of the loop. By developing content, the organization can incorporate Learning and Application to complete the learning loop.

item.title: From Intuition to Intention: My Journey in Project Management
item.description:
Prepared for ADMG 479, Project Management Maturity, for Professor Dr. Laura Williamson. Drafted by Joshua Keyes, June 4, 2025.

Introduction

My journey through the Project Management specialization at Central Washington University has been one of steady growth, practical application, and transformative learning. With a diverse professional background spanning design, compliance, and team leadership, I entered this program with a desire to formalize my management experience and refine my skills in organizational planning, execution, and strategic communication. This reflection paper serves as a capstone for that passage, offering a comprehensive reflection on how this specialization shaped my growth as a project manager.

item.title: ITAM Project Management Internship Reflection
item.description:
1. Learning Objectives

My Spring 2025 internship at an EdTech employer focused on three core learning objectives that guided my development as a systems-oriented project manager and visual communicator. Building on foundational experiences from ITAM 490A, I sought to translate theory into practice by enhancing tool proficiency, streamlining operational workflows, and elevating cross-team communication through visual storytelling.

Objective 1 — Become Proficient with Jira

I deepened my knowledge of Jira by managing sprint cycles, grooming backlogs, and setting up project-specific dashboards. These systems were implemented across both the internal content-system product pipeline and the Upper Elementary microlesson content initiative. I structured Epics and Stories to reflect evolving production workflows, enabling greater task traceability and faster throughput. Dashboards became key tools in daily team syncs, reinforcing accountability and aligning output with strategic objectives.

item.title: Agile Manifesto Values and Principles
item.description: The Agile Manifesto provides a foundational framework for agile project management, emphasizing adaptability, collaboration, and a customer-focused approach. It comprises four core values and twelve principles. Below, each is described and contextualized with examples from personal project experience. An acronym for AGILE proposed by Roula Lombardi is Adapt continually, Groom your requirements and refinements for release of features, Iterate through two-to-four-week scrum cycles, Launch and learn through deployment, and Empower your scrum teams (Lombardi, 2020).

item.title: SMART Goals Progress Report
item.description:
This is a mid-quarter status report on three personal SMART goals, written as a project-manager email to my instructor. Before the report itself I want to note something about the assignment structure, because part of the skill of professional communication is recognizing when the format gets in the way of the message.

A Note on the Assignment Format

A real project manager rarely updates on three unrelated goals in one email. The progress report template in the textbook is built for one project, with one set of milestones, one set of anticipated problems, and one infographic. Stacking three goals into a single template creates an abstraction layer that does not match how a supervisor actually digests progress. The cleaner version of this assignment is one report per goal, three reports total, in priority order. The cleaner version of the infographic is one per goal as well. I am submitting the combined version because that is the assignment, but I want to name the awkwardness on the record. Recognizing when the template is fighting the audience is part of the communication skill the class is teaching.

item.title: Kelvin Education
item.description: At Kelvin Education I serve as Lead Production Designer on a small team building mental-wellness tools for students, in partnership with programs like CalHOPE. Most of the work is proprietary and not shown here. The pieces below are the publicly-released subset — broadcast-style video production, partnership announcements, and the social-media templates the staff use every week to keep the brand voice consistent without a designer in the loop for each post.

item.title: ITAM Internship A Reflection
item.description:
1. Learning Objectives

My first learning objective was to gain experience as an Agile project-management scrum master for the development team. Though I assist in prioritization and help unblock the development team throughout sprints, our team does not ascribe to strict scrum implementation. I also prefer to avoid the scrum points system, as it creates a distraction and an alluring incentive for team members to prioritize the gamified system over the actual applied outcomes. My objective was modified to increase confidence and organization during developer daily standups and bi-weekly meetings. I incorporated weekly one-on-one meetings with individual developers in order to assess and assist with progress and prioritization.

item.title: Internship Week 05: Cloud as Sustainability Strategy
item.description:
1. Sustainability Initiatives at the Organization

Energy Reduction Through Cloud and Remote Work

The EdTech employer saves energy through two structural choices: cloud-vendor infrastructure and a fully remote workforce. By running our application on services like AWS and Heroku, we get secure, scalable servers that are partitioned from other tenants’ data but still co-located on shared physical hardware. That co-location is the point. Greater efficiency is gained when the server assets in use are kept as loaded and as well-maintained as possible, by experts whose entire job is keeping those machines efficient.

item.title: Internship Week 02: Contribution to the Company
item.description:
1. Contribution to the Company

As a project manager at the EdTech employer, I oversee SOC 2 Type 2 certification and lead a website redesign initiative. The SOC 2 Type 2 work is moving slowly but ramping up as we commit to audit dates in collaboration with our SOC 2 auditor. The website redesign has entered phase two. Phase one’s deliverable was confirming that the current site was up to date, compliant, and information-accurate. The redesign itself requires graphic-design collaboration with our design team, and because I have a graphic-design background I was chosen to execute it. The work is being done in Figma, a new tool for me, since past web designs only required Adobe Photoshop. I continue to work on these initiatives, though I have not yet been able to commit the time and attention I would prefer.

item.title: Loyalty Program Project
item.description: A note on naming. The original paper named the specific EdTech employer, its flagship product, the cities and platforms involved, and the CRM and support tools in use. For public posting, all of these have been generalized: “the organization,” “the survey platform,” “the sales CRM,” “the support platform,” “a major cloud productivity suite,” and similar. The analytical content is preserved. The intent was always portfolio reflection on customer relationship design, not commentary on any specific commercial entity.

item.title: Three Facts, Three Questions
item.description: A note on naming. The original paper named the specific EdTech employer, its flagship product, and the device and productivity ecosystem the platform depends on. For public posting, all of these have been generalized: “the organization,” “the survey platform,” and “a major cloud productivity suite and device ecosystem.” The analysis is preserved. The intent was always portfolio reflection on the habit of pre-employment diligence, not commentary on any specific commercial entity.
